qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CORS origin reflection with credentials enables cross-origin data theft and account takeover
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes CORS misconfiguration allowing credential theft through unrestricted origin reflection with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. This is entirely different from the advisory's plugin ZIP upload RCE vulnerability. The advisory specifies CWE-434 (Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type) involving unsafe ZIP extraction of uploaded plugin packages allowing PHP file uploads, while this finding involves improper CORS headers enabling cross-origin requests. Different vulnerability class (CORS vs file upload), different root cause (origin reflection vs unsafe ZIP extraction), different security boundary violation (cross-origin trust vs file type validation).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes an authenticated Remote Code Execution vulnerability in AVideo's plugin upload/import functionality where unsafe ZIP extraction allows authenticated administrators to upload PHP files (CWE-434). The only qualifying finding is CORS misconfiguration enabling credential theft through unrestricted origin reflection, which is an entirely different vulnerability class and root cause. Neither the vulnerability type, affected component (plugin upload vs CORS headers), nor security boundary (file type validation vs cross-origin trust) match the advisory description.
evidence: Advisory specifies CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type in plugin ZIP extraction functionality
Advisory requires authenticated administrator (PR:H) according to the summary and details
Advisory root cause: unsafe ZIP extraction without file type allowlist placing PHP files in web-accessible directory
Qualifying finding involves CORS misconfiguration (PR:N, UI:P) enabling credential theft through origin reflection
Different vulnerability types: file upload RCE vs CORS credential theft
Different root causes: unsafe ZIP extraction vs improper CORS headers
Qualifying finding does not mention plugin upload, ZIP extraction, or file type validation
